question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that flywayHikariConfig.setMaximumPoolSize(10) is hardcoded and cannot be tuned, to cause connection pool saturation during high-concurrency Flyway migration scenarios, blocking all 10 connections and preventing the importer from completing startup, effectively shutting down the node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DataSourceProperties.determineUrl() fallback path (used when connectionDetails is null) to cause the importer to connect to an attacker-controlled database endpoint by manipulating the spring.datasource.url property through an externally accessible configuration mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a deserialization or property binding vulnerability in BlockProperties or RecordDownloaderProperties to flip their isEnabled() return values to true simultaneously, triggering the IllegalStateException in init() and preventing the importer from starting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring Boot Actuator endpoint (e.g., /actuator/env or /actuator/refresh) accessible without authentication to modify the hiero.mirror.importer.block.enabled and hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled properties to both true, causing init() to throw IllegalStateException on the next context refresh and shutting down the importer node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Kubernetes ConfigMap or environment variable injection vulnerability to set both block and record downloader enabled flags to true across multiple importer pods simultaneously, causing all affected pods to fail the init() check and shutting down 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of input validation on importerProperties.getTopicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp() to inject a Long.MIN_VALUE or Long.MAX_VALUE timestamp that causes Flyway SQL migration scripts using the topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp placeholder to violate database constraints, crashing the migration and the importer node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the network name comparison uses equalsIgnoreCase without trimming whitespace, injecting a network value of ' mainnet' (leading space) that fails the MAINNET check and falls through to the non-mainnet timestamp (1588706343553042000L), causing incorrect topic hash versioning on a mainnet node and corrupting consensus data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Flyway HikariCP pool is created with the database owner credentials (dbProperties.getOwner() / dbProperties.getOwnerPassword()), and that these credentials are passed to dbWaiter.waitForDatabase() in plaintext, to intercept or log these credentials through a man-in-the-middle attack on the JDBC connection, enabling subsequent attacks on the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DBProperties.sslMode defaulting to SslMode.DISABLE to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the Flyway database connection, injecting malicious SQL migration responses that corrupt the importer's schema state and cause subsequent processing failures across multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of SSL enforcement (DBProperties.sslMode = SslMode.DISABLE) in the Flyway connection pool to intercept the owner-credential database connection and inject a DDL statement that drops or corrupts a critical table, causing all importer nodes sharing the same database to fail and shutting down 30%+ of processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DBProperties.loadBalance = true setting to redirect Flyway database connections to a malicious load-balanced node that returns incorrect migration state, causing Flyway to skip or re-run migrations and corrupt the importer database schema?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that hikariConfig.copyStateTo(flywayHikariConfig) copies all properties including any attacker-influenced connectionInitSql (from DBProperties.connectionInitSql), injecting a malicious SQL statement that executes on every new Flyway pool connection and disrupts database state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DBProperties.connectionInitSql field (which defaults to empty string but is not validated for malicious content) to inject a SQL command that is executed on every new Flyway HikariCP connection, causing database-level disruption that affects all importer nodes sharing the same database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flyway placeholder injection (configuration.getPlaceholders().put()) to cause a Flyway migration script to execute with an incorrect timestamp boundary, causing the importer to process topic running hash records incorrectly and producing corrupted consensus data that propagates across the network?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Flyway pool uses setMinimumIdle(0) combined with setIdleTimeout(60000) to cause all idle connections to be closed after 60 seconds, and then trigger a burst of Flyway migration activity that requires more than 10 simultaneous connections, causing connection acquisition timeouts and crashing the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @FlywayDataSource annotation to cause Spring Boot's Flyway auto-configuration to use an attacker-supplied DataSource bean instead of the legitimate flywayDataSource(), bypassing the owner-credential setup and causing Flyway to run with insufficient permissions, failing migrations and crashing the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring Cloud Config or external configuration server vulnerability to push a configuration update that enables both block source and record downloader simultaneously, causing init() to throw IllegalStateException on the next application restart and preventing recovery of crashed importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that init() is annotated with @PostConstruct and runs during Spring context initialization to cause a denial-of-service by repeatedly triggering application restarts through configuration manipulation, keeping 30%+ of importer nodes in a crash-restart loop?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Flyway HikariDataSource is created with new HikariDataSource(flywayHikariConfig) without any exception handling, causing an uncaught RuntimeException during pool initialization to propagate up and crash the entire Spring application context, shutting down the importer node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a connection validation query in the Flyway HikariCP pool configuration to cause the pool to hand out stale or broken connections to Flyway migration tasks, causing silent migration failures that corrupt the database schema and prevent the importer from processing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that importerProperties.getNetwork() strips the prefix delimiter (NETWORK_PREFIX_DELIMITER = '-') to craft a network value like '-mainnet' that resolves to an empty string after substringBefore(), causing the MAINNET check to fail and injecting the wrong timestamp into Flyway migrations on a mainnet node?
